summary: Iteration 3: tested all NEW features (real user matching, conversations/messaging, share match) plus regression. Backend: 35/35 pytest tests pass (15 regression in backend_test.py + 20 new in test_new_features.py). Frontend E2E: registered two real users (Alice and Bob) via UI, completed onboarding with skills, verified Dashboard renders Real People cards (10 found, green-bordered, data-testid='real-match-card-*'), Messages and Profile nav buttons render, clicking a real-match card navigates to /match/real-<id>, Share button copies https://.../share/<token> to clipboard and the public /share/:token page renders the shared match card without auth, Start Collaboration creates a conversation and navigates to /messages/:convId, sending a message renders it (2 messages visible), ProfileEdit page name/bio/save all work, /messages list shows the conversation. No critical issues found.

minor.issue: Still uses secure=False, samesite='lax' while /auth/login and /auth/register use secure=True, samesite='none' (carried over from iteration 2).
No rate limiting and no validation on the size/shape of `match` dict - a malicious client could push arbitrarily large JSON; consider a max-size guard.
No max message length enforced; consider capping content length (e.g., 5000 chars).
Iterates all non-admin users (limit 50) in Python and computes set intersections; will not scale beyond a few hundred users. Consider MongoDB $in skill matching with $expr or an aggregation pipeline.

issues: Match card shows 'Hi' bio for users who entered just 'Hi' - expected (data integrity, not a bug). Section title and green-bordered cards look clean.
Right side of empty conversation shows 'Active' as a status but there is no real presence detection - it's a static label. Consider removing or wiring real presence.

action_items: (carryover) Align /api/auth/refresh cookie flags with login/register (secure=True, samesite='none').
(carryover) Clean up markdown-fence regex in generate_matches_with_ai (raw-string double-backslash).
Add max-length guard on POST /conversations/:id/messages content and POST /share/match match payload.
Remove or wire the static 'Active' label in Messages chat header (currently always shows 'Active').
Optional: refactor /api/matches/real to use a MongoDB aggregation pipeline for skill intersection rather than in-memory Python loop.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 776 lines (grew from 536) - strongly recommend splitting into routers/services (auth, profile, matches, messages, share, ai_service, storage_service).

Conversation participants are stored as a sorted list of user_id strings. Good for idempotency. However, list_conversations queries `{'participants': user['id']}` which uses Mongo's implicit array-contains match - works but consider an index on participants.
Messages collection has no index on (conversation_id, created_at); will slow down with scale.
shared_matches has no expiry/TTL; tokens live forever. Consider TTL index or expiry timestamp.

POST /share/match accepts arbitrary `match: Dict[str, Any]`; the public GET returns it verbatim including user_skills and user_name (PII). Acceptable since the user opted in to share, but ensure UI clearly labels this.
send_message awards 'Collaborator' achievement on every message send by iterating in Python and re-writing the entire achievements array - inefficient. Use $set on the specific subdocument or a flag.
No DELETE endpoint for conversations or messages - acceptable for MVP but document as known limitation.
(carryover) MatchDetails.copyToClipboard now wrapped (verified during E2E - share button copied URL and no overlay error). Resolved.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py (new - 20 tests covering real matches, conversations CRUD/auth, share match public access)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (real matches, share clipboard, public share page, start collaboration -> messages send, profile edit, messages list - all verified end-to-end)

test_credentials: admin@skillpartner.com / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). E2E users are created dynamically.
seed_data_creation: None. Test users created at runtime in fixtures and via UI registration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All NEW features (real user matching, conversations/messaging, share match) are working end-to-end - both backend (35/35 pytest) and frontend (Playwright E2E full flow). Reusable backend regression tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py. The only remaining items are minor non-blocking refactors and the carryover /auth/refresh cookie inconsistency from iteration 2. Main agent can ship.
rca of the issue: No bug to RCA - all tests pass. Previously fixed insert_one dict-mutation bug verified working: conversations and messages return without MongoDB _id leakage and serialize cleanly (test_get_messages explicitly asserts '_id' not in msgs[0]).
